# Vanilla JS SPA — contracts for parallel implementation

Read this before writing any layout, widget, page, or shell module. It defines the shapes
and hooks every unit must conform to so the pieces snap together without a merge pass.

## 1. content-index.json (generated by scripts/gen-content-index.mjs, fetched once by js/content.js)

```jsonc
{
  "themes": ["people", "process", "data", "technology"],
  "modules": { "<theme>": ["<moduleSlug>", ...] },
  "chapters": {
    "<chapterSlug>": {
      "title": "", "description": "", "icon": "", "color": "",
      "theme": "", "module": "", "hidden": false, "comingSoon": false,
      "order": 1 // 1-based index within its module (siteOrder unlisted -> 9999+1)
    }
  },
  "topics": [
    {
      "slug": "", "chapterSlug": "", "file": "01-example.md", // ACTUAL filename on disk (may have NN- prefix the slug doesn't have)
      "theme": "", "module": "", "url": "/theme/module/chapter/slug", "chapterUrl": "/theme/module/chapter",
      "order": 1, "title": "", "description": "", "estimatedMinutes": 3,
      "topicLayout": "node-topic|card-grid|comparison|data-table|full-widget|rasci-table|process-step-detail|ui-training|undefined",
      "widget": "", "widgetStep": 0,
      // ...every other frontmatter field verbatim (nodeType, summary, bullets, nodeLocation, lineInLabel,
      // lineOutLabel, durationLabel, transportMode, consumptionLabel, cards, tableColumns, tableRows,
      // left, right, orgChart, roles, steps, inputs, outputs, systems, tasks, screenshot)
    }
  ],
  "adjacency": {
    "/theme/module/chapter/slug": { "prevUrl": null, "prevTitle": null, "nextUrl": "...", "nextTitle": "..." }
  }, // theme-scoped, precomputed exactly like chapters.ts getAdjacentTopics()
  "roles": [ /* RoleConfig + slug + url, sorted by order — see src/lib/roles.ts RoleConfig */ ],
  "learningPhases": [ /* verbatim content/learning-phases.json */ ],
  "glossary": [ /* GlossaryTerm[] with aliases/related/seeAlso defaulted to [] */ ],
  "faq": [ /* FaqEntry[] with seeAlso/related defaulted to [] */ ],
  "topicMap": { "<chapterSlug>/<topicSlug>": { "title": "", "url": "" } },
  "glossaryTerms": [ { "term": "", "aliases": [], "slug": "", "definition": "" } ],
  "configuration": [ { "title": "", "description": "", "order": 0, "screenshot": "", "slug": "", "body": "<pre-rendered sanitized HTML>" } ]
}
```

Ordering, theme/module defaults (`theme ?? 'technology'`, `module ?? 'planning-software'`), and the
9999-for-unlisted rule must reproduce `src/lib/chapters.ts` exactly — the generator is the only place
that logic lives now; `js/lib/chapters.js` just reads pre-sorted data back out.

**Generated HTML artifact:** `content-index.json` carries frontmatter/metadata only — no body content.
Each topic's body is compiled once at generation time (Node-side `marked` + `dompurify`, see §6) and
written to a standalone file on disk at `content/chapters/<chapterSlug>/<topicSlug>.html`, alongside the
source `<sourceFilename>.md` it was compiled from. This file serves two purposes at once: it's a
human-inspectable/diffable generated artifact, AND it's what the app fetches at runtime — at navigation
time `js/pages/topic.js` calls `fetchTopicBodyHtml(chapterSlug, topicSlug)` (in `js/content.js`), which
does `fetch(url(`content/chapters/${chapterSlug}/${topicSlug}.html`))` and returns the response text.
This is a per-topic network request on every topic navigation (not preloaded, not cached across topics).

## 2. Layout render contract (`js/layouts/*.js`)

```js
export function render(props) { /* returns an HTML string */ }
export function afterMount(rootEl, props) { /* optional; called once after the string is injected into the DOM */ }
```

`props` is the same `sharedProps` shape `[topic].astro` builds today (title, description, chapterTitle,
chapterSlug, chapterColor, chapterUrl, topicOrder, chapterOrder, prevUrl, nextUrl, prevTitle, nextTitle,
theme, module, totalTopics) plus the layout-specific frontmatter fields, plus a `bodyHtml` field (already
sanitized HTML, or `''`) for the layouts that have a body slot (`topic`, `full-widget`, `ui-training`).
`bodyHtml` is NOT part of `content-index.json` — it's precompiled at generation time by
`scripts/gen-content-index.mjs` (Node-side `marked` + `dompurify`) into a standalone file at
`content/chapters/<chapterSlug>/<topicSlug>.html` (see §1), and `js/pages/topic.js`'s `buildSharedProps`
fetches it per-navigation via `fetchTopicBodyHtml(chapterSlug, topicSlug)` (in `js/content.js`) before
building `sharedProps`. There is still no client-side markdown parsing or sanitization — the fetched HTML
arrives already-rendered — but it is a real per-topic network request rather than a synchronous read.

**Escaping rule:** `render(props)` returns an HTML *string* that the router assigns via `main.innerHTML =`
— there is no DOM to call `textContent` on at that point, so string interpolation itself is the injection
point. Every frontmatter-derived value interpolated into the template literal MUST be passed through
`escapeHtml()` (imported from `js/markdown.js`) first, e.g. `` `<h1>${escapeHtml(props.title)}</h1>` `` —
this is what Astro's `{expr}` auto-escaping was doing for you at compile time, and it does not happen for
free in a plain template literal. The only field allowed through unescaped is `bodyHtml` (already
DOMPurify-sanitized) — interpolate it directly, e.g. `` `<div data-pagefind-body>${props.bodyHtml}</div>` ``.
If a layout's `afterMount(rootEl, props)` sets text via real DOM APIs, `textContent` is fine there instead.

`layout-registry.js` maps `topic.topicLayout` to a module: `node-topic`, `card-grid`, `comparison`,
`data-table`, `full-widget`, `rasci-table`, `process-step-detail`, `ui-training`, else default → `topic`.

## 3. Widget contract (`js/widgets/*.js`)

```js
export function render() { /* returns an HTML string, static markup, no data in/out */ }
export function init(root) { /* required; (re)binds listeners scoped to `root`, must be idempotent */ }
```

`widgets/registry.js` maps `topic.widget` → module: `what-if`, `demand-shock`, `walkthrough`, `graph`
(takes `topic.widgetStep`, passed as `init(root, { step })`), `demand-flow`, `supply-flow`, `org-chart`
(takes `topic.orgChart`, passed as `init(root, { orgChart })`; uses `org-tree-node.js` for recursion),
`demand-slicing`, `variety-disagg`, `seasonal-disagg`.

## 4. DOM hooks that MUST stay identical (other modules depend on exact selectors)

- `[data-pagefind-body]` — wraps the topic's rendered content; `shell/glossary-tooltip.js` scans only inside this.
- `#nav-back-link` — back-link anchor in topic header.
- `[data-role-nav]` — wraps the prev/next nav block on topic pages.
- `#chapter-tab-bar` — chapter tab strip on chapter-index pages.
- `#chapter-back-overview` — "back to overview" link on chapter-index pages.
- `#complete-btn` / `#complete-label` / `#unclear-btn` / `#unclear-label` with `data-topic-id` on `#complete-btn` — topic-progress.js hooks.
- `#user-dashboard-btn`, `#dash-badge` — dashboard trigger + badge.
- `window.openSearch()`, `window.openIntro()` — globals the fixed icon bar's inline `onclick` calls.
- `#glossary-data` / `#topic-map` — `<script type="application/json">` blocks the shell populates at boot (from `content-index.glossaryTerms` / `.topicMap`) for glossary-tooltip.js / search.js to read.

## 4b. Shell module contract (`js/shell/*.js`)

Two kinds:
- **Fixed-chrome modules** (`site-overlay`, `role-matrix`, `user-dashboard`, `theme-toggle`, `intro-overlay`,
  `search`): export `mount(container)`, called exactly once at boot by `app.js`. They inject their own DOM
  (panels/modals/buttons) into `container` and must self-manage any re-rendering by listening to whatever
  window events they care about internally (e.g. `user-dashboard.js` listens for `platform-progress-changed`
  itself to refresh its stats — `app.js` does not orchestrate this).
- **Per-route behavior binders** (`topic-progress`, `glossary-tooltip`, `role-phase-nav`): export a plain
  function (`initTopicProgress()`, `scanGlossary(container)`, `applyRoleNav(fromParam)`) that the router calls
  after every single route render (see §5) because they bind to DOM that gets replaced on navigation. These
  must be idempotent / safe to call repeatedly — see the `_noteEl` caching pitfall below.

`js/pages/topic.js` is the SPA equivalent of `[theme]/[module]/[chapter]/[topic].astro` — it owns
layout-registry + widget-registry dispatch, adjacency, and role-phase context computation (Phase 2).
The topic body IS fetched per-navigation (via `fetchTopicBodyHtml`, see §1 and §2) but is not
client-side-rendered — it arrives already compiled and sanitized at generation time (see §6), so
`buildSharedProps` (and therefore `render()`) must be `async` to await that fetch before returning props.

## 5. Router lifecycle events

`router.js` only knows about **page modules** (`js/pages/*.js`, one per route, contract: `async render(params, query) -> htmlString`, optional `async afterMount(root, params, query)`). It has no idea what a "layout" or "widget" is — that composition is entirely internal to `js/pages/topic.js`, which is itself just a page module that happens to call `layout-registry.js` + `widgets/registry.js` and returns their composed output, and whose own `afterMount` calls the resolved layout's `afterMount` and the resolved widget's `init`.

After every route render, `router.js` does, in order:
1. Resolve + `import()` the matched page module, call its `render(params, query)`, inject the returned HTML into `#app-main`.
2. Call the page module's `afterMount(root, params, query)` if present (this is where `pages/topic.js` internally triggers the layout's `afterMount` + widget's `init`).
3. Call `shell/glossary-tooltip.js`'s `scanGlossary(document.querySelector('[data-pagefind-body]') ?? root)`.
4. Call `shell/role-phase-nav.js`'s `applyRoleNav(fromParam)` if `?from=` is present in the query string.
5. Dispatch `window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('page-rendered'))`.
6. `shell/topic-progress.js` (via `app.js`) and `shell/user-dashboard.js` (internally) listen for `page-rendered` to (re)bind — never `DOMContentLoaded`, which only fires once for the whole SPA session.

**topic-progress.js port note:** the original caches its note-button/modal DOM nodes in module-level
variables (`_modalEl`, `_noteEl`). `_modalEl` is appended to `document.body` directly, so it's safe to
keep as a singleton across navigations. `_noteEl` is inserted as a sibling of `#unclear-btn`, which is
discarded every time `#app-main` re-renders — **do not cache `_noteEl` across renders**, rebuild it each
time `initTopicProgress()` runs or the note button will end up detached from the DOM.

## 5b. URL handling — index URLs are BASE-free, consumers must prefix

Every URL stored in `content-index.json` (`topic.url`, `topic.chapterUrl`, `chapter` urls derived from them,
`role.url`, FAQ/glossary anchors, etc.) and produced by `js/lib/*.js` is a **route-relative path with no
leading slash and no BASE prefix** — e.g. `"technology/planning-software/01-understanding-basics/item"`,
matching exactly what `router.js`'s `stripBase()` produces when parsing the current location. `js/lib/*.js`
must NOT bake `BASE` into these — that duplicated Astro's `import.meta.env.BASE_URL`-at-build-time behavior,
but here the index is static generated data independent of where it's deployed.

**Whenever a layout, page, or shell module emits an `<a href="...">` (or does `router.navigate(...)`), it
must wrap the path with `url()` from `js/base-url.js` at render time**: `` `<a href="${url(topic.url)}">` ``.
Forgetting this is the single most likely bug across all the parallel ports — every file that renders a
link must import `{ url }` from `'../base-url.js'` and use it on every href.

## 6. Security

Markdown bodies are rendered and sanitized entirely at generation time, under maintainer control —
`scripts/gen-content-index.mjs` runs the npm `marked` + `dompurify` packages in Node and writes the
result to `content/chapters/<chapterSlug>/<topicSlug>.html` (not into `content-index.json`, which carries
no body content). There is no client-side markdown-rendering trust boundary: `marked`/`DOMPurify` are not
shipped to the browser at all (no vendored `js/vendor/*` scripts). The app does `fetch()` these compiled
`.html` files per-navigation (via `fetchTopicBodyHtml`, see §1/§2), but that's a fetch of already-sanitized
HTML, not raw markdown — `js/pages/topic.js` interpolates the fetched `bodyHtml` directly as trusted,
pre-sanitized HTML (see §2).

Frontmatter-derived strings are a separate concern and are NOT pre-rendered HTML — they're plain strings
that layouts interpolate into template literals themselves, so they remain untrusted at render time:
- Every frontmatter string: `textContent` / `escapeHtml()` (from `js/markdown.js`), never raw `innerHTML`.
